_about: TI.1/D2.1 numeric VGA timing + framebuffer-mapping contract for the rev B Video card. Single source of truth consumed by the chip-level twin (revb_video_card_ttl.v), the scanout checker, and the netlist generator. Do not restate these numbers elsewhere. Horizontal units = dot clocks; vertical units = lines. Standard IBM/VESA 640x480@60 (25.175 MHz), which is what mengstr/TTL640x480 (commit ea1ecd0) generates.

framebuffer._note: VJUGA source is a 320x241 monochrome bitmap: 40 bytes/row x 241 rows = 9640 bytes at 0xD800, 8 horizontal pixels per byte, MSB-first (matches cosim's vram.bin rendering and the facts file).

scanout._note: 320x241 source doubled x2 => 640x482, which is 2 lines taller than the 480 active raster. The crop-vs-letterbox choice is resolved by the oracle at TI.2 and frozen into 'vertical_fit' (candidates below).
scanout.vertical_fit_resolved: TI.2, against cosim/vram.bin at the banner: content is top-anchored (rows 0..149 used), source rows 239-240 blank, so dropping the 241st row loses nothing for the banner (the B2 exit content). revb_video_crop_check.py enforces row 240 is blank. Residual: if full-screen firmware ever writes row 240, its bottom 2 VGA lines are cropped (bench-visible; flip to letterbox here if it matters).

vertical_fit_candidates.crop_bottom: draw doubled rows 0..479; drop the last 2 doubled lines (source rows 240.5-241)
vertical_fit_candidates.letterbox: center 482 rows in 480 by clipping 1 doubled line top+bottom
vertical_fit_candidates.letterbox_black_top: blank ~pad lines top/bottom, draw 240 doubled source rows (480) fully

dac._note: Monochrome: one video bit -> R=G=B via a resistor DAC; 75-ohm terminations at the DSUB. A single 'on' level (approx 0.7V into 75R) is enough; a 2-bit-per-channel ladder is optional headroom for a later greyscale mode.
